title: The Silk Road
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The Silk Road was (0) not a single road, but rather a network of trade routes connecting the East and West. For over 1,500 years, it served as the main conduit for commerce, culture, and ideas between Asia and Europe.||The route got its name (9) from the lucrative silk trade that originated in China, although many other goods (10) were also transported along these paths, including spices, precious metals, and gemstones. Merchants would travel for months, crossing deserts and mountains, (11) to reach distant markets. The Silk Road (12) was not only important for trade but also played a crucial role in spreading religions, technologies, and artistic styles.||Cities along the route, (13) such as Samarkand and Bukhara, became wealthy cultural centres where different civilizations met and exchanged ideas. However, the Silk Road's importance declined (14) after the 15th century when maritime routes were developed. Despite this, its legacy (15) has endured, shaping the development of many societies. Today, historians continue to study the Silk Road to better understand (16) how ancient civilizations interacted and influenced each other across vast distances.

example.text: The Silk Road was (0) a single road, but rather a network of trade routes.

questions.explanation: Expresión 'got its name from' indica el origen del nombre.
Voz pasiva pasada: 'were transported' con sujeto plural.
Infinitivo de propósito: 'travel to reach' indica el objetivo.
Verbo copulativo pasado: 'was not only important'.
Expresión 'such as' introduce ejemplos de ciudades.
Preposición temporal 'after' indica posterioridad al siglo XV.
Presente perfecto: 'has endured' indica persistencia hasta ahora.
Adverbio interrogativo 'how' introduce una cláusula sobre el método.
